item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This article has been disqualified from the contest at hand.

item.title: Potential Error
item.content:encoded:
"Posted signage around the perimeter of and and"

Did you mean to simply say "and."

EDIT: Sorry, looking at an old version of the document. Error was fixed in the new version.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ooh… it's the Anglerforest…

If you go down to the woods today, you're sure of a big suprise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you for seeing that! I fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
JSYK, there's a small, white arrow on the left side of figure A that looks like it's not supposed to be there.

+1 by the way

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
it's a reference to the album cover for the life of pablo by kanye west

Ahh, I see. I thought I'd seen the color scheme and style somewhere. tbh I'm pretty sure I only have Love Lockdown and Humble edit which is by KL so ignore me

I don't want to crowd the technical bit with another explanatory footnote, and I'm not the first person to use ARI (though a browse through a search returns a handful of my articles as almost half of the total results, I now see, so I may be more fond of the concept than most) so I'm not sure I want to do anything to that one. For the other sentence, 'gleaned' does mean 'extracted information', but I suppose it's not a common way to say it. So I went ahead and changed it to 'obtained'. Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(it's a reference to the album cover for the life of pablo by kanye west)

most of my problems with the sentences are that they're overly complicated for my tiny brain.

for example

aetheric resonance imaging indicates a high concentration of thaumaturgic particles

this took me out of it because i had to stop and figure out what all those words meant

also,

However, this is based upon context gleaned from the document included in Addendum II and cannot be objectively confirmed.

i'm not sure if gleaned is the right word here? i don't know how to improve it specifically but i think extracted or maybe even just taken would work.

if you don't agree with any of this and/or want to keep it, that's fine with me. i already upvoted and that's probably not going to change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm afraid I don't get whatever reference is within your recreation of the image, there… sorry. I'm glad you liked it, though! (Also, if you'd like to point out the awkward sentences, the editing would likely be minor enough to do during the contest with no issue.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is a cool skip! i enjoy it. the prose can be a bit weird at times but whatever.

the image reminded me of something, though...
the image reminded me of something, though...

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thank you to WrongJohnSilver and Gabriel Jade for feedback. Extra thanks to Gabe for giving me early concept feedback that saved me from wasting more time than I did on my first draft. Clichés used are:

  • Compulsion effect that makes you do bad things/kills you
  • (arguably, if applied to 4434 itself or its -C component) Spooky scary murder monsters
  • D-Class = Disposable Class

One image is here and is Attribution 2.0 Generic. The edit I made (which must be noted) was blurring the bottom edges of the image. The other image is my editing and my photography.
